name: Blueberry Citrus Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and lightly flour two 8‑inch or 9‑inch round cake pans.
3. In a large bowl, blend the cake mix, eggs, oil, water, and orange juice. Beat on medium speed for 2 full minutes.
4. Gently fold in the blueberries. Pour batter into pans.
5. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until done.
6. Cool on a rack for 10 minutes, then invert cakes (blueberry side up) and cool completely.
Citrus Frosting:
1. In a separate bowl, blend softened butter and cream cheese until fluffy.
2. Beat in powdered sugar and orange juice.
3. In another bowl, beat whipping cream until soft peaks form.
4. Blend whipped cream into frosting and add grated orange and lemon zest.
5. Beat until combined and frost the cake as you normally would for a two‑layer cake (blueberry side up).
